data.description: Hand a SUB-TASK to another registered agent and GET ITS ANSWER BACK so YOU fold it into your own reasoning (you stay in control; this is NOT a final user reply and NOT a take-over). Pick a peer by id (mios-a2a-delegate --list-peers) or by skill name (--list-skills). Use when you want a second agent's result; to PASS THE SESSION to another agent use transfer_session_to_agent.
data.name: a2a_delegate

data.description: SEMANTIC search over the installed-app inventory: given a fuzzy natural-language description ('my note-taking app', 'phone settings'), return the top matching installed apps {name, category, launch, score}. Use when you need to FIND which app matches a vague description. For the FULL installed-app+game list use list_installed_apps; to actually launch use launch_app.
data.name: app_search

data.description: Gracefully CLOSE an OPEN Windows window by title (sends WM_CLOSE so the app saves state). Force-kill is intentionally unsupported. Use to close / quit / dismiss a window -- not to hide it (use minimize_window to send it to the taskbar).
data.name: close_window

data.description: Write a PYTHON snippet that ORCHESTRATES MULTIPLE MiOS verbs programmatically instead of emitting one verb call at a time: `import mios_tools` exposes the verb surface (mios_tools.web_search(...), .system_status(), .call('<verb>', ...)). Runs in a rootless podman sandbox (no network, read-only, dropped caps). Use when a task needs several tool calls glued with logic/loops -- NOT for a one-off shell command (run_sandboxed_code).
data.name: code_mode

data.description: Run a SHORT one-off code snippet / command in an isolated sandbox (bubblewrap: read-only system, writable scratch only, NO network by default, resource caps + timeout). lang: bash (default) | python. Pass net=true only if it must reach the network. Use to compute/test something safely WITHOUT touching the host -- not to orchestrate MiOS verbs (run_python_tool_script) or to run Windows PowerShell (powershell_run).
data.name: coderun

data.description: RESTART ONE podman/quadlet CONTAINER by name. For podman CONTAINERS -- NOT systemd services (use service_restart). Write-class.
data.name: container_restart

data.description: Check podman/quadlet CONTAINER status. Empty name = all containers. For podman CONTAINERS -- NOT systemd services (use service_status) and NOT whole-machine health (system_status). Read-only.
data.name: container_status

data.description: Fetch ONE web page you already have the URL for and return its FULL content as clean, LLM-ready MARKDOWN via the LOCAL crawl4ai engine (headless Chrome over CDP, with a Camoufox stealth-Firefox fail-retry; offline-first, no cloud). The default reader for a single long ARTICLE or DOC page (~20000 chars). Does NOT fetch_url_markdown multiple pages / a whole site. For news/article INDEX pages use fetch_index_markdown; for quick short plain text use fetch_url_text.
data.name: crawl

data.description: Find a LINUX desktop UI element via the AT-SPI accessibility tree by its role/name and return its screen coordinates -- semantic a11y-tree lookup, NO pixels/vision. Read-class. Use when you can name the control (its label/role); use linux_desktop_locate_element for a fuzzy/visual description that may need the vision model.
data.name: cu_atspi_query

data.description: Move the pointer to (x,y) on the LINUX (Wayland/Hyprland) desktop inside the MiOS VM and click. This clicks the in-VM Linux screen, NOT the host Windows screen (use windows_desktop_click for that). Write-class, approval-gated. button = left|right|middle.
data.name: cu_click

data.description: Locate a LINUX desktop UI element from a NATURAL-LANGUAGE description and return {x,y,confidence}. Tries the AT-SPI a11y tree first, then falls back to a vision model (qwen3-vl / UI-TARS) on a screenshot. Read-class. Use for fuzzy 'find the blue Submit button'; use linux_desktop_find_element_by_name when you know the exact label.
data.name: cu_ground

data.description: Press ONE key (Enter, Tab, F5, Escape, ...) on the LINUX (Wayland/Hyprland) desktop inside the MiOS VM. Single key only -- use linux_desktop_press_key_combo for modifier chords like Ctrl+S. Targets the in-VM Linux session, NOT the host Windows desktop (use windows_desktop_press_key). Write-class.
data.name: cu_key

data.description: Press a MODIFIER CHORD (e.g. Ctrl+S, Alt+Tab, Ctrl+Shift+T) on the LINUX (Wayland/Hyprland) desktop inside the MiOS VM. Use this for any combo with modifiers; use linux_desktop_press_key for a single bare key. Targets the in-VM Linux session, NOT the host Windows desktop. Write-class.
data.name: cu_key_combo

data.description: Capture the LINUX (Wayland/Hyprland) desktop inside the MiOS VM to a PNG file (one-shot). This is the Linux in-VM screen, NOT the host Windows screen. Read-class. Pair with linux_desktop_click / linux_desktop_locate_element to act on what you see.
data.name: cu_screenshot

data.description: Type literal text into the focused surface on the LINUX (Wayland/Hyprland) desktop inside the MiOS VM. Sends keystrokes to whatever window has focus in the in-VM Linux session, NOT the host Windows desktop (use windows_desktop_type_text). For editing FILES, prefer create_file / replace_file_text instead. Write-class, approval-gated.
data.name: cu_type

data.description: List the open top-level windows on the LINUX (Wayland/Hyprland) desktop inside the MiOS VM (via wmctrl / hyprctl / AT-SPI). This is the in-VM Linux desktop, NOT the host Windows windows (use list_windows for those). Read-class. Returns window titles/handles for the Linux session.
data.name: cu_window_list

data.description: FAST first-try 'where is X' lookup over a cached filesystem map (~19k entries via mios-daemon, refreshed every 15 min, sub-100ms). TRY THIS FIRST for 'where is X' / 'find the file X' / 'what's the path to X' before the slower per-OS scans (windows_file_search, linux_file_search). Locates existing files/dirs on THIS machine -- not a web or knowledge search. May miss very new/uncached files.
data.name: directory_lookup

data.description: Actually POST a message to the operator's Discord (local bot, default channel). Use to DELIVER a message when the operator asks to post / send / share / DM / 'let me know on' Discord -- it really posts and returns the message id, so never claim something was sent without calling this. For a DM to a specific user, pass the user parameter with their @username or Discord user ID. One-shot send (for repeating posts use schedule).
data.name: discord_send

data.description: Author a NEW document FROM TEXT YOU WRITE -> a real office artifact (.docx/.pptx/.xlsx/.pdf/.html). Output format inferred from out_path extension; write markdown (or CSV rows for .xlsx with from_fmt=csv); for .pptx separate slides with a line of ---. Fully local (Pandoc/LibreOffice). Use this to GENERATE a doc from content; to convert an EXISTING file use convert_document_format.
data.name: docgen_build

data.description: Convert an EXISTING file to another format (.docx->.pdf, .md->.docx, .csv->.xlsx, .pptx->.pdf). Target format inferred from out_path extension. Fully local via Pandoc / LibreOffice headless. Use this to TRANSFORM a file that already exists; to author a NEW doc from text you write use create_office_document.
data.name: docgen_convert

data.description: Search for files on the WINDOWS side of this machine via the Voidtools Everything NTFS index (exhaustive, name/extension match). Finds FILES that already exist on Windows drives (C:, etc.) -- NOT a web or knowledge search, and NOT the Linux filesystem (use linux_file_search for that). Good for 'where is X.exe' on Windows; use find_file_fast first for a quick cached hit.
data.name: everything_search

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='install', backend='flatpak', id=...)]
data.name: flatpak_install

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='list', backend='flatpak')]
data.name: flatpak_list

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='preflight', backend='flatpak', id=...)]
data.name: flatpak_preflight

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='search', backend='flatpak')]
data.name: flatpak_search

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='show', backend='flatpak', id=...)]
data.name: flatpak_show

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='uninstall', backend='flatpak', id=...)]
data.name: flatpak_uninstall

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='upgrade', backend='flatpak')]
data.name: flatpak_upgrade

data.description: Raise an already-OPEN Windows window to the FRONT (by title) and optionally re-position it (same placement enum as launch_windows_app). Use to switch to / bring forward / activate a window that already exists -- it does NOT launch anything. Run list_windows first to confirm the title.
data.name: focus_window

data.description: Search for files on the LINUX side of this machine (plocate -> locate -> find; exhaustive name/extension/path match). Finds FILES on the Linux filesystem -- NOT a web or knowledge search, and NOT Windows drives (use windows_file_search for C:/Windows paths). Use find_file_fast first for a quick cached hit; fall back here for a full disk scan.
data.name: fs_search

data.description: TRANSFER (take-over) the live session to ANOTHER agent: the target inherits the shared session state via the A2A contextId and CONTINUES the workflow in your place. Use when a DIFFERENT model should TAKE OVER from here (e.g. hand a heavy task down to a lightweight CPU agent). This passes control away; to get a sub-answer back while staying in control use delegate_subtask_to_peer_agent.
data.name: handoff

data.description: INGEST local files/notes INTO the notes vault (the offline second-brain) -- LOCAL sources only, no cloud/SaaS. Walks a directory (or one file), tiers each doc (L0 abstract + L1 overview via the light model), and stores it as a vault node so notes_vault_list/search/read can navigate it and recall can surface it. Idempotent. This is a WRITE that adds documents to the vault, not a search.
data.name: ingest

data.description: Query the OWUI RAG knowledge COLLECTIONS (curated document corpora uploaded to Open WebUI) and return citation-ready hits {score, source, snippet}. This is the RAG document store -- distinct from the local notes vault (notes_vault_search, your second-brain of skills/Q&A/memory), from durable AI memories (recall), and from web/file search.
data.name: knowledge_search

data.description: Enumerate the currently OPEN Windows-desktop windows (titles). Run this BEFORE focus/move/close to confirm the target window exists. NOT the installed-app inventory (use list_installed_apps) and NOT the Linux/Wayland desktop (that is linux_desktop_window_list).
data.name: list_windows

data.description: Maximize an OPEN Windows window to fill its containing monitor. To restore it back to normal size use restore_window; for a specific pixel size use resize_window.
data.name: maximize_window

data.description: DELETE a durable memory you previously stored (Letta/MemGPT core_memory_remove) by its `key`. Use when a stored fact is wrong or obsolete and should be removed entirely. Pair: remember (write) / recall (read) / memory_update (edit). Returns {ok, op, key}.
data.name: memory_forget

data.description: EDIT a durable memory you previously stored (Letta/MemGPT core_memory_replace): overwrite the fact at `key` with a new value. Use to CORRECT or refine a stored fact instead of piling up duplicates with `remember`. Requires the existing key. Pair: remember (write-new) / recall (read) / memory_forget (delete). Returns {ok, op, key, updated}.
data.name: memory_update

data.description: Minimize an OPEN Windows window -- hide it to the taskbar without closing it. To bring it back use restore_window; to actually close it use close_window.
data.name: minimize_window

data.description: Full INVENTORY of installed apps AND games across every surface (flatpak/rpm/winget/store/steam/epic/xbox/gog/cli/service). THE verb for 'what apps/games are installed', 'list my programs', 'detect my installed games'. Optional substring filter. NOT a web search, NOT list_windows (open windows only); for fuzzy 'which app does X' use app_search.
data.name: mios_apps

data.description: Resolve an app/program NAME to the exact runnable launch command WITHOUT launching it -- returns the cmd line only; the agent then decides whether to run it (e.g. via launch_windows_app/launch_app). This is NOT a file search (use find_file_fast / windows_file_search / linux_file_search to locate files) and does NOT open anything. Use it to get the precise command before launching, or to check how something would be started.
data.name: mios_find

data.description: Move an OPEN Windows window to a SEMANTIC region: center / left / right / top-left / top-right / etc., optionally on a chosen monitor. Use when the operator names a place ('to the left', 'center it') rather than exact coordinates. For literal pixel (x,y) use place_window_at_pixel; to change size use resize_window.
data.name: move_window

data.description: LAUNCH any app/program by name (resolves Windows AND Linux/flatpak apps) and optionally place its window (position center/left/right/top-left..., monitor index) or pass CLI args. THE one launch verb -- use for any 'open / launch / start / run X'. After launching, confirm with verify_launch.
data.name: open_app

data.description: SHOW a web page to the operator in their VISIBLE browser window (one they look at). Use for 'open / pull up / show me this link in the browser'. NOT for research: to look something up and report the answer back, use a WEB search / page fetch instead -- this just displays the page, it does not return its content.
data.name: open_url

data.description: Run a PRE-DEFINED, NAMED OS-shell recipe from the [recipes.*] catalog (per-OS templates, shell-escaped, WSL path-converted). You pick an EXISTING recipe by name + params -- you do NOT write the code. Enumerate available recipes via `mios-os-recipe list`. For writing arbitrary code use run_sandboxed_code; for Windows PowerShell use powershell_run.
data.name: os_recipe

data.description: Move the cursor to (x,y) on the HOST WINDOWS desktop and click. This clicks the real Windows screen the operator sees, NOT the in-VM Linux desktop (use linux_desktop_click for that). Write-class. button = left|right|middle.
data.name: pc_click

data.description: Click a UI control BY NAME (substring) in the FOREGROUND window on the HOST WINDOWS desktop via UI Automation -- finds the named control and clicks its center, acting on a SEMANTIC target instead of guessed pixels (more robust than pc_click x,y). Prefer this over pixel-clicking when the control has a name; use pc_find_element first if unsure of the exact name. Write-class.
data.name: pc_click_element

data.description: Find UI controls BY NAME (substring) in the FOREGROUND window on the HOST WINDOWS desktop via UI Automation -- the SEMANTIC alternative to guessing pixel coordinates. Returns matching elements with control type, automation_id, bounds, and clickable center. Use to LOCATE a button/menu/field before acting; then pc_click_element (by name) or pc_click (with the returned center). READ-only.
data.name: pc_find_element

data.description: Press a key or modifier chord on the HOST WINDOWS desktop (e.g. Enter, Tab, F5, or Ctrl+S as a combo). Targets the real Windows screen, NOT the in-VM Linux desktop (use linux_desktop_press_key / linux_desktop_press_key_combo). Write-class. This single verb handles both bare keys and modifier combos.
data.name: pc_key

data.description: List ALL named UI controls in the FOREGROUND window on the HOST WINDOWS desktop via UI Automation -- a 'set of marks' SURVEY (each with control type, automation_id, bounds, clickable center, mark number) so you can SEE what's actionable, then pc_click_element (by name) or pc_click (the center). Use when you don't know the control names yet. READ-only.
data.name: pc_list_elements

data.description: Capture the WINDOWS host desktop (the operator's real screen) to a PNG file via the in-session UIA executor. This is the Windows screen, NOT the Linux in-VM desktop (use cu_screenshot for that). Read-class. Pair with windows_desktop_* (UIA) or a vision grounder to act on what you see.
data.name: pc_screenshot

data.description: Type literal text into the focused window on the HOST WINDOWS desktop. Sends keystrokes to the foreground Windows app, NOT the in-VM Linux desktop (use linux_desktop_type_text). For editing FILES, prefer create_file / replace_file_text instead. Write-class.
data.name: pc_type

data.description: INSTALL / SEARCH / SHOW / UPGRADE / UNINSTALL / LIST software packages BY ID across winget (Windows), flatpak (Linux), or dnf. action=search|show|install|upgrade|uninstall|list|preflight; backend=auto picks the OS. This MANAGES packages; for the question 'what apps/games are installed' use list_installed_apps (the inventory). 'list' here = installed PACKAGES, not the app catalog.
data.name: pkg
data.sig: action, id?, query?, backend="auto"?, limit=10?, scope?

data.description: Move an OPEN Windows window to LITERAL pixel coordinates (x, y). Use only when you have exact coords -- call screen_layout first to learn monitor geometry/resolution. For named regions (center/left/right) use move_window_to_region; to change size use resize_window.
data.name: position_window

data.description: Execute a PowerShell script on the WINDOWS side (not the Linux sandbox). High-privilege -- tainted sessions are refused; script cap 64 KiB. Use for Windows-host administration / cmdlets. For sandboxed Linux bash/python use run_sandboxed_code instead.
data.name: powershell_run

data.description: List running OS PROCESSES, optionally filtered by name; sorted by rss (memory) / cpu / pid. Use to see which processes are running and what they consume. For container/service state use container_status / service_status instead.
data.name: process_list

data.description: READ back the durable FACTS you previously stored with `remember` (Letta/MemGPT core-memory read). Returns the agent-authored memories for a scope {ok, count, memories:[{key, scope, fact, source}]}. This is your own persistent memory of facts -- NOT a vault search (notes_vault_search), NOT RAG (knowledge_search). Pair: remember (write) / memory_update (edit) / memory_forget (delete).
data.name: recall

data.description: WRITE a durable FACT to your own persistent memory (Letta/MemGPT core-memory, self-editing). Use when the operator tells you something to keep, or you learn a stable fact worth carrying across turns/conversations. scope: global (default) | agent:<name> | conversation:<id>. Optional `key` lets you later memory_update/memory_forget it. This stores ONE fact -- to bulk-notes_vault_ingest documents use notes_vault_ingest.
data.name: remember

data.description: Resize an OPEN Windows window to a literal pixel size (width x height). This ONLY changes size -- it does NOT move the window. To relocate use move_window_to_region (named region) or place_window_at_pixel (exact x,y); to fill the monitor use maximize_window.
data.name: resize_window

data.description: Restore an OPEN Windows window from a minimized OR maximized state back to its last normal-size geometry. Use to 'un-minimize' / 'un-maximize' / bring back a window. Pairs with minimize_window and maximize_window.
data.name: restore_window

data.description: Schedule a RECURRING/standing task: run <prompt> through the agent on a repeating interval and post each result to the operator's Discord. Use for ANY 'do X every N minutes/hours', 'each day', 'keep me updated on ...', 'check ... regularly' request -- a STANDING job, NOT one-shot research.
data.name: schedule

data.description: Report Windows MONITOR geometry: each display's resolution, position, and which is primary. Use BEFORE place_window_at_pixel so you know valid coordinates. This describes the SCREENS/monitors, not the open windows (use list_windows for those).
data.name: screen_layout

data.description: Gracefully RESTART ONE Linux systemd SERVICE by unit name. For systemd services -- NOT podman containers (use container_restart). Note: for MiOS-owned services prefer the mios-restart recipe which preserves state. Write-class.
data.name: service_restart

data.description: Check ONE Linux systemd SERVICE: is-active state + a status snapshot for the named unit (e.g. mios-agent-pipe, hermes-agent). For systemd services -- NOT podman CONTAINERS (use container_status) and NOT whole-machine health (use system_status). Read-only.
data.name: service_status

data.description: TIERED summarisation of a document/text (the L0/L1/L2 gating primitive): L0 = one-sentence ~100-token abstract, L1 = ~2k-token structured overview (sections + key points), L2 = the raw text (returned as a char count, fetched on demand). Pass `text` or a `file` path. Runs on the CPU light lane. This CONDENSES content you supply -- it does NOT search or store anything (use notes_vault_ingest to persist).
data.name: summarize

data.description: READ the cached system/environment inventory (shared store): identity, the host OS + whether this is WSL2 + the Windows host, host hardware, which MiOS stack services are up/down, loaded ollama models, and the installed launchable-app inventory by category. Read this to know the ENVIRONMENT and WHAT IS INSTALLED/RUNNING instead of guessing the OS or the apps. Cached (may be slightly stale -> sys_env_refresh). Read-only.
data.name: sys_env

data.description: WRITE/UPDATE the cached environment inventory: re-probe apps + services + models + host and overwrite the shared sys_env_snapshot database. Call when you suspect the sys_env_snapshot cache is stale (e.g. just installed/removed an app or started a service). This is the refresh half; sys_env_snapshot is the read half.
data.name: sys_env_refresh

data.description: Read recent LOG / journal entries from systemd. Filter by unit (mios-agent-pipe / hermes-agent / mios-open-webui / ...) or level (err / warning / info). Default: 50 lines from the last 10 minutes. Use to debug what a service printed -- NOT for live health numbers (system_status) or service up/down state (service_status).
data.name: system_logs

data.description: Live LOCAL machine health dashboard (JSON): OS (Linux distro + kernel + whether this is WSL2 + the WINDOWS HOST OS caption/version when on WSL2), GPU/VRAM, RAM, disk usage, running services, loaded ollama models. THE verb for 'system status', 'system health', 'what OS / Windows version am I on', 'is the GPU/memory/disk ok'. Local state only -- NEVER a web search, and NEVER answer the OS/host from training data: read it HERE (the `os` field). Single source of truth for these live numbers.
data.name: system_status

data.description: CREATE a NEW file on disk with the given text content (path + content). For making a file that does not yet exist; to change an EXISTING file use replace_file_text or insert_file_lines, and to read one use read_file. Refuses write-protected system paths (/etc, /usr, /boot, /mnt/c/Windows, /mnt/c/Program Files).
data.name: text_create

data.description: INSERT new text into an existing file AFTER a given 1-indexed line number (0 = prepend to the very top); all existing content is kept. Args: path, line, content. Use this to ADD text at a position WITHOUT deleting anything; to OVERWRITE/replace existing text use replace_file_text, and to make a brand-new file use create_file.
data.name: text_insert

data.description: Find-and-replace text inside an existing file: replace an EXACT substring (old) with new text (new). `old` must occur EXACTLY once -- supply a larger surrounding block if it is ambiguous. Use this to MODIFY/overwrite existing content; to ADD lines without deleting use insert_file_lines, and to make a new file use create_file.
data.name: text_str_replace

data.description: READ the contents of a local file (returns text with 1-indexed line numbers, optional start/end range) OR list the entries of a local directory if path is a folder. This is for files/folders ALREADY on disk -- it does NOT search the web and does NOT find files by name (use windows_file_search on Windows or linux_file_search on Linux first to locate a path). For Windows paths, resolve via windows_file_search FIRST.
data.name: text_view

data.description: Find VERBS/TOOLS in the catalog by natural-language query when no listed verb obviously fits the user's ask. Returns top-k {name, sig, desc, score} via embedding search over verb name+description. Use to DISCOVER a capability you don't see; not for searching the web (web_search), files (windows_file_search/linux_file_search), or knowledge (knowledge_search).
data.name: tool_search

data.description: CHECK whether an app/window ACTUALLY launched, read-only, in-turn (delegates to the daemon-agent's Definition-of-Done window check). Call with the SAME name AFTER you fired launch_app / launch_windows_app / focus_window to confirm before claiming success. This only CHECKS -- it does NOT launch; to launch and confirm in one go use launch_and_verify_app.
data.name: verify_launch

data.description: READ ONE node from the local notes vault at a chosen detail TIER (hierarchical gating): l0 = one-line abstract, l1 = structured overview (generated on read), l2 = raw full content (most context, fetch only when truly needed). `node` is `skills/<file>` or `knowledge/<id>` obtained from notes_vault_list / notes_vault_search. This reads a vault node, NOT a disk file (read_file) and NOT a web page.
data.name: viking_cat

data.description: SEMANTIC search across the LOCAL notes vault (the offline second-brain: self-learned skills + stored Q&A + AI memory + ingested notes). Matches your query against L0 abstracts and returns hits {id, l0}; then notes_vault_read the best at a deeper tier. This searches YOUR local vault, NOT the OWUI RAG document collections (knowledge_search), NOT the web, NOT files on disk.
data.name: viking_find

data.description: LIST nodes in the local notes vault (the offline second-brain) at the cheap one-line (L0) tier for navigation. Pick a namespace: `skills` (self-learned SKILL.md files), `knowledge` (every finished Q&A), `memory` (stored AI memories). Skim these abstracts FIRST, then notes_vault_read the right node at a deeper tier. This is the local vault index, NOT web/file/RAG search.
data.name: viking_ls

data.description: Fetch ONE web page you already have the URL for and return its readable ARTICLE TEXT (lightweight, ~4000 chars, plain text not markdown). Use after web_search to read a single promising result and quote what it actually says. For full clean MARKDOWN of a long doc use fetch_url_markdown; for a news/article INDEX page use fetch_index_markdown. If the page lacks the answer, say so; never invent.
data.name: web_extract

data.description: Fetch ONE web page by URL to clean, LLM-ready MARKDOWN via the LOCAL self-hosted Firecrawl engine (offline-first, no cloud). A second markdown fetch engine alongside fetch_url_markdown; its verified strength is NEWS / ARTICLE INDEX and LISTING pages (homepages, news fronts, blog/forum indexes) where the markdown of links + headlines must render reliably -- prefer it for those. For a single long article/doc page use fetch_url_markdown; for quick readable plain text use fetch_url_text.
data.name: web_scrape

data.description: WEB search via the LOCAL self-hosted SearXNG (offline-first metasearch, no cloud dependency). USE THIS for anything from the internet or the current world: weather, news, current events, event dates/times/locations, prices, products, people, places, scores, facts, general knowledge -- ANY question whose answer is not a file on this machine. Returns real fetched {title, url, content} plus instant answers/infoboxes for citation. This is NOT file search: windows_file_search/linux_file_search/find_file_fast ONLY find files on disk and return nothing useful for a knowledge/current-events question. When a question needs a live fact you don't already have, call THIS and ground on the result -- never invent the answer, never default to remembered facts. RECENCY: when the answer must be CURRENT (today/latest/trending/breaking/recent/this week/this month), set time_range='day' (or 'week'/'month') so SearXNG returns fresh dated results instead of evergreen encyclopedia or year-overview pages; you may also set category='news' for breaking headlines. Leave time_range and category empty (null) for timeless facts.
data.name: web_search

data.description: Change the STATE of an OPEN Windows window (by title): minimize (hide to taskbar), maximize (fill its monitor), restore (back to normal size), or close (graceful WM_CLOSE so the app saves state). Run list_windows first to confirm the title. This is state only -- to bring a window to the front use focus_window, to relocate use move_window_to_region / place_window_at_pixel, to change size use resize_window.
data.name: window_state

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='install', backend='winget', id=...)]
data.name: winget_install

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='list', backend='winget')]
data.name: winget_list

data.description: [legacy: use pkg(action='search', backend='winget')]
data.name: winget_search
